key_insight: HiReLC uses a two-level reinforcement learning controller to jointly prune and quantize neural networks, combining local per-block decisions with global budget allocation guided by sensitivity estimates.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers an architecture-agnostic way to automate model compression, making it useful for reducing storage and compute costs in deployment pipelines.
method: Low-level agents choose per-kernel settings for bitwidth, keep-ratio, quantization type, and granularity.
High-level agents allocate compression budgets across blocks using ensemble voting and Fisher Information sensitivity.
An active-learning loop with a lightweight surrogate reduces the cost of RL evaluation and fine-tuning.
evidence_signal: Across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks, the method achieved about 5.99–6.72× compression with accuracy changes ranging from a 3.83% gain in one setting to 0.55–5.62% drops elsewhere.
watch_item: The surrogate is only for reward shaping, so real gains still depend on expensive post-compression fine-tuning and careful validation on each target architecture.
paper_title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
